question: When did Stellar Protocol 26 "Yardstick" go live on Mainnet, and which CAPs define its main changes?

golden.answer: Protocol 26, Yardstick, went live on Stellar Mainnet on May 6, 2026 after the validator vote. Its included changes were CAP-0073 (SAC management of G-account balances), CAP-0077 (validator-governed freezing of ledger entries through network configuration), CAP-0078 (bounded TTL-extension host functions), CAP-0079 (muxed-address StrKey conversions), CAP-0080 (additional efficient BN254/ZK host functions), CAP-0081 (TTL-ordered eviction), and CAP-0082 (checked 256-bit integer arithmetic). Do not collapse Yardstick's BN254 work into Protocol 25: Protocol 25 introduced BN254/Poseidon primitives, while CAP-0080 extended the BN254 host-function set in Protocol 26.
keyFacts: Protocol 26 Yardstick went live on Mainnet on May 6, 2026.
Names CAP-0073, 0077, 0078, 0079, 0080, 0081, and 0082 with materially correct roles.
Distinguishes CAP-0080's Protocol 26 extensions from the BN254/Poseidon primitives introduced in Protocol 25.
avoid: Do NOT say the May 6, 2026 date was only a testnet upgrade or an uncompleted plan.
Do NOT attribute all BN254 and Poseidon support first introduced on Stellar to Protocol 26.
golden.notes: Historical protocol membership is stable. CAP registry labels such as Accepted or Implemented are not substitutes for the dated network-activation source.

corroboration.claim: Protocol 26 Yardstick went live on Mainnet on 2026-05-06

evidence.note: SDF states that Yardstick went live on Mainnet on May 6 following the validator vote.
CAP preamble binds the marquee freeze mechanism to protocol version 26.

corroboration.claim: Protocol 26 includes CAP-0073, 0077, 0078, 0079, 0080, 0081, and 0082 with the summarized roles

evidence.note: CAP preamble identifies Protocol version 26 and the proposal title/behavior.
